title: HTML for Email Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential rules for creating HTML email templates.

questions.question: Which of these is the preferred and most compatible way to create layouts for HTML emails?
options: Flexbox
CSS Grid
HTML Tables (<table>)
Divs with float attributes

questions.question: Why is it mandatory to use inline styles (style='...') for almost all CSS in email templates?
options: Inline styles load faster.
Many email clients strip out the <style> tag in the <head>.
They are required by the Gmail API.
They are better for search engine optimization within the email.

questions.question: Which of these is considered a 'web-safe' font that is correctly supported in most email clients?
options: Arial
Inter
Roboto
Open Sans

questions.question: Should you rely on background images to convey critical information in an email?
options: Yes, they look great in all modern clients.
No, many clients like Outlook have poor support for background images.
Only if you use PNG format.
It depends on the user's screen resolution.

questions.question: What is the result of many users having images disabled by default in their email clients?
options: The email will not load at all.
The design will be broken, but text will remain readable if you use 'alt' attributes.
The browser will automatically enable them.
It triggers a popup to the user to enable them manually.
